question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the TopicContext.startTime is set to DomainUtils.now() at construction time (not at subscription time) to create a race condition where isComplete() evaluates endTime against a stale startTime, causing submitted transactions near the boundary to be incorrectly included or excluded?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.justOrEmpty(entityRepository.findById(topicId.getId())) call in topicExists() to perform a cache-timing attack against the entity cache, determining whether a topic ID has been recently accessed and inferring transaction activity patterns?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on the subscribeTopic() endpoint to perform a resource exhaustion attack by opening thousands of subscriptions with startTime=0, each triggering a full historical scan via topicMessageRetriever.retrieve(), overwhelming the database with concurrent queries?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that missingMessages() does not validate that the recovered messages actually fall within the gap (startTime to endTime), allowing the retriever to return messages outside the expected range that are then injected into the stream as if they were legitimately submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnSubscribe(s -> subscriberCount.incrementAndGet()) and doFinally(s -> subscriberCount.decrementAndGet()) callbacks to cause a counter imbalance by canceling subscriptions at precise moments, making the Micrometer gauge report incorrect subscriber counts and masking a transaction stream interception attack?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the startTime = last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 calculation in incomingMessages() to cause a one-nanosecond gap between the last historical message and the live stream start, allowing a submitted transaction with consensusTimestamp exactly equal to last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 to be missed if it arrived between the historical and live phases?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(() -> incomingMessages(topicContext)) lazy evaluation in subscribeTopic() to cause incomingMessages() to be called after a significant delay, during which submitted transactions may have been processed by the historical retriever but not yet reflected in topicContext.getLast(), causing them to be re-fetched by the live stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageServiceImpl.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gap recovery filter in missingMessages() uses the original filter's topicId without re-validating it, allowing a scenario where the topicId has been reassigned or deleted between the initial subscription and the gap recovery query, causing messages from a different topic to be injected into the stream?
